sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.rawLine: The Dance Major is a liberal arts degree which takes a broad approach to studying dance as: a movement practice, a performance activity, a choreographic enterprise, a social practice, a cultural practice, a creative endeavor, a subject of scientific inquiry, a subject of historical inquiry, and an agent of sociopolitical change. The curriculum includes study in a variety of genres, including African and African-Diasporic dance, Western concert dance, somatic practices, and social dance. Embodied learning is brought into dialogue with artistic, scientific, and humanistic modes of inquiry throughout the curriculum, which is designed for students with diverse backgrounds and interests. Students have opportunities to engage in academic or creative research, perform, choreograph, and contribute to community projects. Dance majors can elect to pursue an honors track.

The curriculum includes study in a variety of genres, including African and African-Diasporic dance, Western concert dance, somatic practices, and social dance. Embodied learning is brought into dialogue with artistic, scientific, and humanistic modes of inquiry throughout the curriculum, which is designed for students with diverse backgrounds and interests. Students have opportunities to engage in academic or creative research, perform, choreograph, and contribute to community projects. Dance majors can elect to pursue an honors track. requirementCueLines: The Dance Major is a liberal arts degree which takes a broad approach to studying dance as: a movement practice, a performance activity, a choreographic enterprise, a social practice, a cultural practice, a creative endeavor, a subject of scientific inquiry, a subject of historical inquiry, and an agent of sociopolitical change. The curriculum includes study in a variety of genres, including African and African-Diasporic dance, Western concert dance, somatic practices, and social dance. Embodied learning is brought into dialogue with artistic, scientific, and humanistic modes of inquiry throughout the curriculum, which is designed for students with diverse backgrounds and interests. Students have opportunities to engage in academic or creative research, perform, choreograph, and contribute to community projects. Dance majors can elect to pursue an honors track.
Applicants who meet the following requirements are accepted during autumn, winter, and spring quarters on a rolling basis.
Successful completion of 3 credits in any of the academic courses required for the major.
Core Courses (17 credits): DANCE 150, DANCE 166, DANCE 242, DANCE 271 (1 credit), DANCE 493 (5 credits)
Dance Studies Courses (6-10 credits): Must include at least one diversity (DIV) course. See adviser for list of approved courses.
Career Transition Course (3-5 credits): DANCE 480 or DANCE 494
Dance Electives (6-12 credits): to reach minimum 38 credits in core, dance studies, dance electives, and career transition courses (or approved alternatives)
Technique Courses: Minimum 18 credits from list of approved courses, representing at least four different movement idioms.

The curriculum includes study in a variety of genres, including African and African-Diasporic dance, Western concert dance, somatic practices, and social dance. Embodied learning is brought into dialogue with artistic, scientific, and humanistic modes of inquiry throughout the curriculum, which is designed for students with diverse backgrounds and interests. Students have opportunities to engage in academic or creative research, perform, choreograph, and contribute to community projects. Dance majors can elect to pursue an honors track.
Applicants who meet the following requirements are accepted during autumn, winter, and spring quarters on a rolling basis.
Successful completion of 3 credits in any of the academic courses required for the major.

Dance Studies Courses (6-10 credits): Must include at least one diversity (DIV) course. See adviser for list of approved courses.
